_comment: ICE CCF-01 Phase 1 governed hook wiring (cc-0026). All guards are wired in --log-only (dry-run) mode; none is enforcing. Bootstrap is inform-only (SessionStart, no enforcing mode). This file is committed/governed; the existing sql-content-gate wiring stays in settings.local.json and is intentionally NOT duplicated here. Enforcing flips happen one guard at a time, each behind a PK gate, after >=3 clean dry-run sessions per guard (cc-0026 §D).
_permissions_comment: READ-ONLY allowances only. Deliberately absent: mcp__supabase__execute_sql (stays in settings.local.json `ask` — the same tool serves SELECT and DML, and permission matching cannot distinguish them; allowing it would punch a hole through the apply_migration/deploy_edge_function hard stops). Also absent: mcp__ccd_session_mgmt__send_message (the harness always prompts for it by design; it cannot be allowlisted). Every entry below is non-mutating. NOTE: Bash(python scripts/db-read.py:*) is the ICE R0 operator read path (scripts/db-read.py) — a SELECT-only wrapper over the Postgres role `ice_readonly`, which is confined by schema-USAGE to 10 curated secret-free `ice_ro` views and CANNOT write, reach schemas m/c, or call any writer function (proven live 2026-07-19: raw-credential write attempts all blocked SQLSTATE 42501). It is the safe-to-allowlist replacement for read-only execute_sql; execute_sql itself STAYS gated in settings.local.json `ask` as the R1 path for rare writes / raw-table reads.
